# Azion Docs — secure

> All pages of the secure section. Root index: https://www.azion.com/en/llms.txt

- [Explore Azion Secure products](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/): Azion's Secure products guarantee the security, availability, and authenticity of your application's data.
- [Azion Bot Manager](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/bot-manager/): Protect your applications against automated attacks such as bad bot signatures or scripted bots.
- [Azion Bot Manager reference](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/bot-manager/reference/): Complete reference for Bot Manager: features, bot classification, report log fields, JSON Args setup options, engine versions, and additional resources.
- [Azion Bot Manager Lite](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/bot-manager/reference/lite/): Protect your applications against automated attacks such as bad bot signatures or scripted bots with a simplified version of the Bot Manager.
- [Certificate Manager](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/certificate-manager/): Certificate Manager is the Secure product where you register and manage the TLS certificates that enable HTTPS traffic for your domains at Azion.
- [Certificate Manager concepts](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/certificate-manager/concepts/): Understand the TLS certificate options at Azion — Azion SAN, edge certificates, Let's Encrypt, and Trusted CA — and how issuance, renewal, revocation, and validation work in Certificate Manager.
- [How to configure mTLS](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/certificate-manager/guides/mtls/): Learn how to configure mTLS in your application at Azion.
- [Let's Encrypt Wildcard Certificates on Azion Platform](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/certificate-manager/guides/wildcard-lets-encrypt/): Technical documentation on using Let's Encrypt wildcard digital certificates to simplify certificate management on the Azion platform with automation, security, and scalability.
- [Certificate Manager quickstart](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/certificate-manager/quickstart/): Register your first TLS certificate, associate it with a domain, and confirm your application is serving HTTPS traffic, using Azion Console or the Azion API.
- [Support for mTLS for Secure](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/certificate-manager/reference/mtls/): Understand how mTLS works at Azion and why to use it.
- [Custom Pages](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/custom-pages/): Custom Pages is a functionality that allows you to customize error pages for users based on the status code received from the edge connectors.
- [DDoS Protection](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/ddos-protection/): Azion DDoS Protection defends your content and applications against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks.
- [DDoS Mitigation](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/ddos-protection/reference/ddos-mitigation/): At Azion, DDoS Mitigation is performed by DDoS Protection, an Firewall add-on.
- [Edge DNS](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/edge-dns/): Edge DNS is an authoritative DNS service that hosts your domains, zones, and records on Azion's distributed infrastructure, with DDoS protection and real-time record distribution.
- [Edge DNS concepts](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/edge-dns/concepts/): Understand how Edge DNS resolves queries: authoritative hosting, Simple and Weighted record policies, wildcard matching priority, TTL and propagation, and DNSSEC authentication.
- [How to look up DNS servers with Dig command](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/edge-dns/guides/run-the-dig-command/): Learn how to specify DNS servers with the dig command. Step-by-step guide for Windows and other systems, including Google DNS queries and record lookups.
- [Diagnose Issues with the Traceroute Command](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/edge-dns/guides/run-the-traceroute-command/): Traceroute is a network diagnostic tool, that our technical support use to diagnose performance and delivery issues.
- [Edge DNS quickstart](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/edge-dns/quickstart/): Create your first zone and record on Edge DNS and confirm that Azion's nameservers answer your DNS queries, using Azion Console or the Azion API.
- [DNSSEC Compatibility](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/edge-dns/reference/dnssec-compatibility/): Azion Web Platform is compliant with the DNS Security Extensions (DNSSEC) specification supporting its use in Azion's accelerated sites and applications.
- [Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/): Azion Firewall is a security product that protects from the network layer to the application layer.
- [Firewall concepts](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/concepts/): Understand how Firewall works: how modules unlock criteria and behaviors, how Rules Engine processes rules sequentially, where functions run, and how network lists are evaluated at the edge.
- [How to access addresses through a root domain (ANAME)](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/access-root-domain/): Configure your Edge DNS to allow access to an address through its root domain.
- [How to activate DNSSEC](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/activate-dnssec/): Use DNSSEC compatibility to guarantee the authenticity and integrity of an IP address response.
- [How to add records](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/add-records/): Add record to your Edge DNS domain.
- [How to block Tor exit node IP addresses](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/block-tor-networks/): Configure a behavior using Rules Engine to block all requests coming from IPs contained in the Azion IP Tor Exit Nodes network list.
- [Acquire & Register a Digital Certificate](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/certificate-manager/): Add a certificate to your account to increase security and reliability.
- [Configure the TLS Cipher Suite for HTTPS](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/ciphers/): Security attacks can exploit particular TLS cipher vulnerabilities; you can select which ciphers your HTTPS application supports.
- [How to create a WAF rule set](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/create-waf-rule-set/): Create rule sets on Web Application Firewall (WAF).
- [How to configure Edge DNS main settings](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/edge-dns-configure-main-settings/): Customize your Edge DNS domains.
- [Understand Edge DNS metrics](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/edge-dns-understand-metrics/): Understand how you can continuously monitor your applications through Azion Observe products.
- [How to configure Firewall main settings](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/firewall-configure-main-settings/): Customize your firewall domains and modules.
- [Protect your domain](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/firewall-protect-your-domain/): Learn how to configure a firewall to protect your domain.
- [Understand WAF metrics](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/firewall-understand-metrics/): You can continuously monitor your applications through Azion Observe products.
- [Azion Firewall — multi-layered security in Edge](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/firewall/): For online service providers like e-commerce, internet banking and media businesses.
- [Automate security with Azion Functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/functions/): Through Functions, you can write your own security source code in JavaScript and deploy it to run at the edge of the network.
- [How to update Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/how-to-update-your-firewall/): Learn how to update features and configurations of Firewall.
- [How to instantiate functions in your firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/instantiate-functions/): Instantiate serverless functions directly within your firewall.
- [How to integrate WAF with SIEMs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/integrate-siems/): Learn to integrate SIEMs with Azion Web Application Firewall (WAF). Enhance threat detection and response capabilities for superior security posture.
- [Add a TXT Record for Let's Encrypt](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/lets-encrypt-record/): When creating a Let's Encrypt certificate externally, such as using Let's Encrypt official tool Certbot, for a hostname that is hosted in Edge DNS, you must add the TXT record required for the DNS challenge.
- [How to perform a load balance between DNS records](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/load-balance-dns/): Perform a DNS load balance by assigning different weights to records configured in Edge DNS.
- [How to manage bots](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/manage-bots/): Protect your applications and its contents from bots attacks with a Marketplace integration.
- [Configure & Associate an mTLS Certificate](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/mtls/): mTLS is a method that validates the digital certificate on the client side and on the edge.
- [Post-quantum cryptography support on Azion Platform](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/post-quantum-cryptography/): Azion Platform provides native support for post-quantum cryptography through TLS 1.3 with ML-KEM hybrid key exchange, protecting against future quantum computing threats.
- [Automate security with Azion SDK](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/sdk/): The Azion SDK for Go serves as a powerful developer toolkit designed to streamline the integration of Azion's API services into Go programming language applications.
- [Automate security with Azion Terraform Provider](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/terraform/): The Azion Terraform Provider is an open source project, registered in HashiCorp. Through the Azion SDK for Go, the provider communicates with the Azion APIs, so you can manage your infrastructure hosted on the Azion platform, locally, as code.
- [How to test an Edge DNS zone processing](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/test-zone/): You can test your zones to check if they're properly processing information.
- [How to tune WAF](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/tune-waf/): Filter possible attacks in your WAF and create an allowed rule from the results you receive.
- [Create Firewall Rules with Rules Engine](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/guides/work-with-rules-engine/): Integrate rules in your firewall to set tasks to be executed for specific scenarios without changing your existing code.
- [Firewall quickstart](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/quickstart/): Create your first firewall, protect a domain, and add a rule that blocks unwanted requests, using Azion Console or the Azion API.
- [Functions Instances for Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/reference/functions-instances/): By activating the Functions module, you can instantiate serverless functions in your Firewall at Azion, as well as set up the conditions for its execution.
- [Functions for Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/reference/functions/): By using Functions in JavaScript on your Firewall you can boost your protection, have more dynamism, and apply the logic that your business requires.
- [Network Lists](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/reference/network-shield/network-lists/): Discover how Azion's Network Lists provide granular control over network layer protection allowlists, blocklists, and graylists with IPs, ASNs and countries.
- [Rules Engine for Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/firewall/reference/rules-engine/): Implement security logic, based on criteria and behaviors, with Rules Engine for Firewall.
- [Network Shield](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/network-shield/): Explore Azion's Network Shield for robust firewall security. Safeguard your infrastructure with granular control and advanced filtering.
- [Create IP, ASN & Geo Blocklists](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/network-shield/guides/blocklists-ip-addresses-edge/): Manage lists of IPs, ASNs, and geolocations from accessing your applications at the edge with Network Lists.
- [Secure Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/overview/): Secure your applications and domains with edge computing protection.
- [Web Application Firewall](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/): Protect your applications against threats like SQL Injections, Remote File Inclusion (RFI), Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), and others with Azion Web Application Firewall.
- [WAF concepts](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/concepts/): Understand how Azion Web Application Firewall works: the score-based detection model, sensitivity levels, Learning and Blocking modes, execution through Rules Engine, and how WAF parses POST requests.
- [How to configure a WAF Custom Allowed Rule](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/guides/configure-waf-allowed-rules/): Configuring a WAF Custom Allowed Rule allows you to instantiate specific rules for the needs of your application.
- [How to check your WAF mode](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/guides/how-to-check-your-waf-mode/): Learn how to check and change the operation mode of WAF configurations.
- [How to find the score of WAF blocked requests](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/guides/how-to-find-waf-score/): Learn how to find more information on WAF blocked requests with Azion.
- [Mitigating CVE-2025-29927: Next.js Middleware Authorization Bypass](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/guides/mitigate-cve-2025-29927-nextjs/): Learn how to protect your Next.js applications against CVE-2025-29927, a critical authorization bypass vulnerability, by configuring a Rules Engine rule in Azion Applications to strip the x-middleware-subrequest header.
- [Configure WAF Rules for a Specific Cookie](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/guides/waf-rules-for-specific-cookie/): With this solution, your web application firewall can accurately filter and manage incoming requests based on specified cookies.
- [WAF quickstart](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/quickstart/): Create your first WAF Rule Set, apply it to a firewall with a Rules Engine rule, and confirm WAF is analyzing and blocking threats, using Azion Console or the Azion API.
- [WAF Exceptions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/reference/custom-allowed-rules/): Optimize the security of your application with Exceptions for WAF. Create, manage, and automate exception rules via API or Console.
- [WAF Rule Sets](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/secure/waf/reference/rules-set/): Define your rule set for protection against threats with Azion Web Application Firewall (WAF).
